description:
Three coupled gaps in the bl-be5f core scaffolding, all from the §13 invariant 'all wire transfer goes through sync; prime is an orchestrator of syncs, not a reimplemented fetch':

(A) prime does NOT fetch current state. My prime runs the prime CHAIN (tracker prime/pre = adopt/found/stealth-lock) but never invokes sync, so on an established/joined checkout `bl prime` adopts ('leave for sync') and then nothing syncs — task state is not pulled current. Spec §12/§13: 'prime gets currency by INVOKING sync, not by reimplementing a fetch'; 'on an already-joined checkout prime is exactly the syncs you'd run by hand plus the idempotent worktree pass.' Fix: prime should drive checkout::sync (the terminus, and per materialized pointer) after substrate + the prime chain.

(B) origin autodiscovery is misplaced + inert. Core's origin_of reads the XDG OPERATING repo's origin, which nothing ever sets -> always None. Per §12 the landing->origin hop is the CODE repo's origin. Move origin autodiscovery into the TRACKER, resolving `git remote get-url origin` from binding.invocation_path (the working dir / code repo); drop core's origin_of. Tracker remote resolution then = pointer > autodiscovered-origin(invocation_path).

(C) `bl sync <ref>` should PULL that specific repo/branch. §6/§13: '<ref> is a pointer-name (landing/terminus) or ANY synced repo.' My impl treats the arg as a branch name on the landing (operating unchanged) — it does not resolve/fetch a given <url>. sync <url> should fetch that remote; sync <hop> should target that hop's local checkout.

Related to bl-02c3 (materialization). May split A (core) / B+C (tracker + sync target). Surfaced by bl-be5f review.
title: prime drives sync for currency; tracker origin autodiscovery; sync <ref> pulls a repo
